title: T-078 — import Permission.ORG_ACCESS_ADMIN from @kira/rbac instead of inlined string (FT-018)
prompt:
# T-078 — import Permission.ORG_ACCESS_ADMIN from @kira/rbac instead of inlined string (FT-018)

PRD: `docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §3
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`
Original carry-forward ID: **FT-018** (from `_meta/audits/2026-05-07-foundation-final-carry-forward-backlog.md`)
Severity: **P2** (XS effort)

## Goal
Replace string 'org.access.admin' with Permission.ORG_ACCESS_ADMIN constant.

## Implementation contract
1. Ensure packages/rbac/src/permissions.ts exports `Permission.ORG_ACCESS_ADMIN = 'org.access.admin'`.
2. apps/web/app/api/flags/route.ts imports Permission and uses the enum.
3. ESLint rule (or grep test) forbids inlined permission strings in apps/web.

## Files (scope)
- apps/web/app/api/flags/route.ts [modify]
- packages/rbac/src/permissions.ts [modify]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the flags route, when scanned, then `Permission.ORG_ACCESS_ADMIN` is used and no bare 'org.access.admin' string remains.
2. Given the rbac package, when consumed, then Permission.ORG_ACCESS_ADMIN === 'org.access.admin'.

## Test strategy
- RED: extend a permission-strings regression test that greps apps/web for bare permission strings; should fail before the swap.
- Run `pnpm test permissions`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not change the underlying string value of the permission.

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED-first) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.
